nodes.goal: Load Niterói roads (+ surface/parks/water) from the local PBF and emit them downstream.

nodes.content:
import io
import requests
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
from rasterio.io import MemoryFile
from rasterstats import zonal_stats

# `arg` is the layer array emitted by the upstream autk-grammar (data-only) node:
#   [{ "name": "table_osm_roads", "type": ..., "geojson": {...} }, ...]
# The grammar loads OSM in EPSG:3395 (its default workspace CRS).
layers = arg if isinstance(arg, list) else [arg]
roads_layer = next(l for l in layers if l.get("name") == "table_osm_roads")
roads = gpd.GeoDataFrame.from_features(roads_layer["geojson"]["features"])
roads = roads.set_crs("EPSG:3395", allow_override=True)

# 24-band Niteroi land-surface-temperature raster (summers 2001-2024).
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/urban-toolkit/autark/main/usecases/public/data/niteroi_lst_verao_2001_2024.tif"
resp = requests.get(url, timeout=180)
resp.raise_for_status()

with MemoryFile(resp.content) as mf:
    with mf.open() as src:
        nbands = src.count
        affine = src.transform
        nod = src.nodata
        # 1 km buffer around each road, in metric EPSG:3395, then to the raster CRS for sampling.
        buf = gpd.GeoSeries(roads.geometry.buffer(1000), crs="EPSG:3395").to_crs(src.crs)
        series = [[] for _ in range(len(buf))]
        for b in range(1, nbands + 1):
            band = src.read(b).astype("float64")
            stats = zonal_stats(buf, band, affine=affine, stats="mean", nodata=nod)
            for i, s in enumerate(stats):
                m = s.get("mean")
                series[i].append(float(m) if m is not None else 0.0)

# Per-road yearly LST series consumed by the downstream grammar OLS compute as an array attribute.
roads["lst_timeseries"] = series
roads = roads.set_crs("EPSG:3395", allow_override=True)
roads.metadata = {"name": "niteroi_roads_lst"}
return roads
nodes.goal: Fetch the 24-band LST raster and attach each road's per-year mean LST within 1 km as lst_timeseries.
Per-road OLS regression of the 24-year LST series on the GPU — output warming angle and intercept.
Colour roads by warming angle in 3D.

nodes.content: {"plot": {"dataRef": "upstream", "mark": "scatter", "axis": ["intercept", "angle"], "title": "LST regression — warming angle vs baseline (Niterói roads)", "events": ["brush"]}}
nodes.goal: Brushable scatter of warming angle vs LST baseline; brushing highlights roads on the 3D map.

dataflow.name: Autark spatial join + regression
dataflow.description: Per-road warming trend in Niterói: a grammar node loads roads from a local PBF, a Python node samples a 24-band (2001-2024) land-surface-temperature raster to build each road's yearly mean-LST series within 1 km, and a second grammar node fits an OLS regression per road on the GPU (warming angle + intercept), rendering a thematic map linked to a brushable scatter. The raster sampling stays in Python (the grammar has no raster source); the regression runs in the grammar's compute block.
